comments.body:
Hello, thank's for this tutorial, I was really in need of this.

I only have one question: how can I use this same podman created by the podman machine, from my WSL Ubuntu?

Thank's in advance!
Sorry for the noise, i removed a trailing quote and rebased against main

> Hello, thank's for this tutorial, I was really in need of this.
> 
> I only have one question: how can I use this same podman created by the podman machine, from my WSL Ubuntu?
> 
> Thank's in advance!

You're welcome. On the Ubuntu instance, you could create an equivalent podman connection that uses the assigned ssh port pointing to localhost and that should work. However a problem you are going to have is that the podman packages are fairly old and running on 3.x instead of 4.x which are not fully compatible. So you most likely need to build your own podman remote. Once you have that build you can copy your private key /mnt/c/users/<your username>/.ssh/podman-machine-default to ~/.ssh/ (dont forget to set perms to 600), and then create a remote connection with podman system connection add --identity ~/.ssh/podman-machine-default podman <ssh url with port you retrieve from podman system connection ls>
@n1hility I've dumped it into a google doc, I'll send you a link when I'm done.  I've a hard time reviewing a multi-page doc in GitHub.
@n1hility I sent the google doc, nothing major, just a few nits that were scattered about.  Holler if you have trouble getting to it.

hey @TomSweeneyRedHat. No thats fine, actually funny enough thats how I wrote the pre-markdown source. 

I applied your changes. thanks!

In case it's easier for folks to review the final merges, the rendered output is here:

https://github.com/n1hility/podman/blob/win-docs/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md
This looks great, but would it be worth publishing this on redhat.com/sysadmin, or has that already been done for the most part?

FWIW, I've had a blog on hold waiting for the bugs to be fixed in the install piece.  I was going to run through it one more time this week and try to get it published.  It's mostly done, I just need to see ti work, adjust, and then also point at this doc file and thank Jason profusely for all  his efforts.
And @n1hility mucho, mucho apologies.  I told you "rootfull" yesterday.  That's wrong, we went to one el, "rootful".  Sorry!
@TomSweeneyRedHat no problem just converted it back to rootful
